threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it ‘the tube’ instead of ‘the Overground' and look surprised when it actually runs on time.
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded a pigeon with an acoustic guitar. No one batted an eye; just another Tuesday in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a family grilling at Paul-Linke-Ufer while a busker played an accordion. That mix of cultures and sounds—pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that stays open till 5 AM, serving currywurst with a side of techno vibes. It's a rite of passage!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know to avoid the Civic Center Muni stop on a Thursday afternoon—always a dog on the tracks or some evacuation drama.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 27 last night, and a guy was juggling flaming batons while the driver just shrugged. Classic Tenderloin—where chaos has a routine.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars are for partying, but many locals prefer the quiet corners of Plaça del Nord for a laid-back chat.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still ask for 'a cafe con leche' at the bar instead of just pointing to the menu.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you ask for directions to the nearest tube and everyone just stares, then points you toward the bus stop.
Spotted a guy on the 137 who spent the whole ride trying to convince a pigeon to sit on his shoulder. Only in Clapham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of kids turning an abandoned lot into a makeshift skate park, laughing like they'd just won the lottery.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can start your day with a coffee from a 24-hour Späti, then stumble into an all-night club before breakfast. Only in Kreuzberg, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction when a bus is late; they’re not surprised. Out-of-towners act like it’s a personal offense.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a whiff of fresh pastries while waiting for the 5 at UN Plaza, only to watch it zoom past. Classic Muni moment—better luck next time!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals eat paella; in Gràcia, you'll find way more kebab shops than traditional places. It's all about the late-night bites.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their eager faces at the Mercat de la Llibertat—trying to haggle over prices of everything, even the fruit.
